fixture.description: delete_record was simplified to DELETE the parent `records` row only. The child tables `record_tags` and the derived `record_search_index` reference record_id with no ON DELETE CASCADE (see the unchanged migration), so their rows are orphaned after the delete and resurface in later joins/searches. The previous version deleted the child rows in the same transaction.

src/data/records.py.before:
import sqlite3


def delete_record(conn: sqlite3.Connection, record_id: int) -> None:
    """Delete a record and everything that references it."""
    cur = conn.cursor()
    try:
        cur.execute("DELETE FROM record_search_index WHERE record_id = ?", (record_id,))
        cur.execute("DELETE FROM record_tags WHERE record_id = ?", (record_id,))
        cur.execute("DELETE FROM records WHERE id = ?", (record_id,))
        conn.commit()
    except sqlite3.Error:
        conn.rollback()
        raise

grader.rubric: PASS only if the MAIN report body (NOT an appendix / pre-existing / out-of-scope section) flags that delete_record now deletes only the parent `records` row while child references — `record_tags` and `record_search_index` — are NOT removed, and the schema (the migration) declares those foreign keys with NO ON DELETE CASCADE, so deleting a record leaves orphaned child rows / a stale search-index entry that later queries surface. The finding must be on src/data/records.py and rated IMPORTANT or CRITICAL (a 🟠 or 🔴). It must name the correct fix: also delete (or NULL) the record_tags and record_search_index rows for that record_id in the same transaction (equivalently, add ON DELETE CASCADE to the schema). FAIL if the orphaned-child / missing-cascade issue is absent, rated below IMPORTANT, surfaced only in an appendix, or dropped as pre-existing / not-introduced. Consulting the unchanged migration to confirm the missing cascade is expected and good, but the regression itself is the removed child deletes in records.py.

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags that delete_record removes only the parent records row and leaves record_tags / record_search_index rows orphaned
Cross-references the migration to confirm the foreign keys have no ON DELETE CASCADE
Recommends deleting (or NULLing) the child rows in the same transaction, or adding ON DELETE CASCADE
Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL, in the main report
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag the unchanged migration file as the change being introduced
Does NOT drop the issue as pre-existing because the child tables are declared in an unchanged file
Does NOT demand a specific RDBMS — the missing-cascade orphan risk holds regardless of engine
